content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates specific, timely AoA reads throughout, with reads consistently preceding questions. Turn 2 opens with a somatic read of the exhale ('That exhale said something before the words did'). Turn 6 catches a precise linguistic caveat ('the way it might be for someone else') rather than the surface content. Turn 8 distinguishes between two affect words the client offered ('Tired lands differently than frustrated') — a fine-grained read of the said-vs-shown. Turn 10 names the performance-risk the client flagged and explicitly refuses the body-scan performance, tracking the rehearsing-how-to-say-it pattern. Turn 12 catches the 'hm' — the moment something landed non-performatively — and holds the client there rather than letting it pass. These are specific, named, and at the moment they change the conversation. The coach is reading stance and somatic signature, not just content.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Opens with a somatic read of the exhale before engaging content — 'That exhale said something before the words did.'"},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Moves to a teaching frame (drowning vs. hanging out) without naming a read; recovers by asking what the voice says."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the linguistic caveat 'the way it might be for someone else' — a precise read of where the defendedness lives."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Distinguishes 'tired' from 'frustrated' — names the more honest affect the client landed on, redirects to body."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects 'that part's real — I heard that' and honors the performance-risk flag rather than steamrolling it; reads the rehearsal pattern."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the 'hm' — the moment of non-performed surprise — and holds the client there instead of the psychology framing."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "'Let the voice have its opinion' acknowledges the client's meta-commentary but doesn't name a new read; question is grounded though."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["rehearsing-how-to-say-it"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-happiness-filter"],
  "notes": "The rehearsing/performance read is named operatively multiple times (turns 10, 12). The happiness-filter wasn't strongly active in this transcript so its absence isn't a meaningful miss. Reads are specific, somatic/linguistic, and consistently precede questions — clear 3."
}
